top_stories.title: Palo Alto Networks integrates CyberArk, Koi and Portkey and launches Idira to bolster AI and identity security
top_stories.summary: Palo Alto Networks completed the acquisition of CyberArk in February 2026 and began integrating CyberArk's privileged access management capabilities into its Cortex and Strata platforms. The company also launched a new product called Idira alongside integrations with Koi and Portkey to bolster AI and identity security.

top_stories.title: Hackers bypass SonicWall VPN MFA due to incomplete patching
top_stories.summary: Threat actors bypassed MFA on SonicWall Gen6 SSL-VPN appliances by brute-forcing VPN credentials, exploiting incomplete patching to gain unauthorized access. The attackers used this access to deploy ransomware-related tools, highlighting a failure in MFA enforcement on unfully-patched SonicWall devices.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Self-Service Password Reset abused in Azure data theft attacks
top_stories.summary: A threat actor is abusing Microsoft Self-Service Password Reset (SSPR) and other legitimate Azure administration features to steal data from Microsoft 365 and Azure production environments. The attacks leverage built-in identity and access management functionality rather than exploiting software vulnerabilities, targeting enterprise cloud environments.

top_stories.title: The New Phishing Click: How OAuth Consent Bypasses MFA
top_stories.summary: A phishing-as-a-service platform called EvilTokens launched in February 2026 and compromised over 340 Microsoft 365 organizations across five countries within five weeks. The attack method uses OAuth consent flows and device code phishing to bypass MFA, tricking users into authenticating at a legitimate Microsoft URL while handing over OAuth tokens to attackers.

top_stories.title: How Storm-2949 turned a compromised identity into a cloud-wide breach
top_stories.summary: A threat actor tracked as Storm-2949 conducted a cloud-wide breach by leveraging compromised credentials and moving laterally through cloud systems without deploying malware. The attack demonstrates how identity compromise alone can enable large-scale data theft by exploiting trusted authentication and access pathways.

news.title: Identity Alone Isn't Enough: Why Device Security Has to Share the Load
news.summary: Specops Software published an article arguing that identity verification alone is insufficient for Zero Trust security, particularly against stolen session tokens and compromised devices. The piece advocates for continuous device verification as a necessary complement to identity checks in Zero Trust strategies.

news.title: Verizon DBIR: Vulnerability exploitation is the dominant initial access vector
news.summary: The 2025 Verizon Data Breach Investigations Report found that vulnerability exploitation has surpassed stolen credentials as the most common initial access vector for the first time in the report's 19-year history. This marks a significant shift from credential-based attacks, which have historically dominated as the primary method attackers use to gain entry into target networks.

news.title: Agent AI is Coming. Are You Ready?
news.summary: Orchid Security released its "Identity Gap: Snapshot 2026" report on May 19, 2026, examining unmanaged identity elements in enterprise environments. The report finds that "identity dark matter" — unseen and unmanaged identity components — now accounts for 57% of enterprise identity landscape, outweighing visible/managed elements, with Agent AI adoption cited as a compounding factor.

news.title: GitHub Breached — Employee Device Hack Led to Exfiltration of 3,800+ Internal Repos
news.summary: GitHub is investigating unauthorized access to its internal repositories after threat actor TeamPCP listed GitHub source code and internal organization data for sale on a cybercrime forum, with over 3,800 internal repos reportedly exfiltrated via a compromised employee device. GitHub states there is currently no evidence of impact to customer data stored outside its internal repositories.

news.title: CISA Exposes Secrets, Credentials in 'Private' Repo
news.summary: CISA inadvertently exposed secrets and credentials in a GitHub repository that was publicly accessible since November 2025. The repository, ironically named "Private-CISA," contained sensitive credentials that should not have been publicly available.

news.title: Compromised Nx Console 18.95.0 Targeted VS Code Developers with Credential Stealer
news.summary: A compromised version of the Nx Console extension (version 18.95.0) was published to the VS Code Marketplace, targeting developers with a credential stealer. The malicious extension, which has over 2.2 million installations, was designed to steal credentials from developers using VS Code, Cursor, and JetBrains editors.

news.title: Popular GitHub Action Tags Redirected to Imposter Commit to Steal CI/CD Credentials
news.summary: Threat actors compromised the GitHub Actions workflow "actions-cool/issues-helper" by redirecting all existing repository tags to a malicious imposter commit. The attack targets CI/CD pipeline credentials, harvesting and exfiltrating sensitive credentials to an attacker-controlled server.

news.title: CIRT insights: How to help prevent unauthorized account removals from AWS Organizations
news.summary: The AWS Customer Incident Response Team published insights on preventing unauthorized account removals from AWS Organizations, based on patterns observed during active security incident responses. The guidance addresses threat actor tactics that exploit specific AWS configuration weaknesses related to organizational account management and access controls.

news.title: Azure Files Entra-Only identities: Advancing cloud-native identity and security
news.summary: Microsoft has announced the general availability of Entra-Only identities for Azure Files SMB, enabling identity-based access to SMB file shares using cloud-native Microsoft Entra ID authentication. This eliminates the previous requirement for on-premises Active Directory or hybrid identity configurations when accessing Azure Files over SMB.

news.title: EMA Research Finds Legacy IAM Systems Are Slowing AI Adoption in Financial Sector
news.summary: EMA Research, commissioned by Ory Corp., surveyed 271 IT and security professionals and found that legacy IAM systems are hindering AI adoption in the financial and insurance sectors. Full-scale AI production deployment in financial institutions trails the broader market at 29.7% versus 40.6%, with IAM infrastructure identified as a key bottleneck.

news.title: Automation Anywhere Collaborates with Cisco, NVIDIA, Okta, and OpenAI, Launching EnterpriseClaw to Run Next-Generation AI Agents Inside Enterprise Systems
news.summary: Automation Anywhere announced a collaboration with Cisco, NVIDIA, Okta, and OpenAI to launch EnterpriseClaw, a platform for running AI agents across cloud, desktop, and on-premises enterprise systems. Okta is listed as one of several partners in the initiative, with the platform focusing on AI agent orchestration and governance rather than identity management specifically.

news.title: Critical Microsoft Vulnerabilities Doubled: From Exposure to Escalation
news.summary: Microsoft critical vulnerabilities doubled year-over-year in 2025 despite a steady total vulnerability count, according to BeyondTrust analysis. The report focuses on attackers' increasing use of privilege escalation and identity abuse as primary attack vectors.

news.title: MajorKey Technologies Selected to Deliver DDIL Identity Capabilities at the Tactical Edge
news.summary: MajorKey Technologies has been selected by a U.S. Department of Defense agency to deliver its NomadID solution for identity capabilities in disconnected, denied, intermittent, and limited (DDIL) environments. NomadID is designed to provide continuous, secure access management at the tactical edge where traditional network connectivity is unavailable or contested.

news.title: Two-Thirds of Nonhuman Accounts Are Unseen and Unmanaged, According to Orchid Security's Identity Gap Report
news.summary: Orchid Security released its Identity Gap Report finding that two-thirds of nonhuman accounts in enterprises are unseen and unmanaged. The report highlights "identity dark matter" as a growing risk, with implications for enterprise identity posture and AI agent readiness.

news.title: 'Claw Chain' Vulnerabilities Threaten OpenClaw Deployments
news.summary: Patched vulnerabilities dubbed "Claw Chain" in the OpenClaw AI agent framework allowed attackers to steal credentials, escalate privileges, and maintain persistence. The flaws affect deployments of the rapidly growing AI agent framework, touching on core IAM concerns including credential theft and privilege escalation.

news.title: Developer Workstations Are Now Part of the Software Supply Chain
news.summary: Three coordinated supply chain attack campaigns targeting npm, PyPI, and Docker Hub were discovered within a 48-hour window, all focused on stealing credentials and access tokens from developer environments and CI/CD pipelines. The attacks targeted API keys, cloud credentials, SSH keys, and tokens, positioning developer workstations as a vector for compromising trusted software access.

news.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, SAP, VMware, n8n Patch RCE, SQL Injection, Privilege Escalation Flaws
news.summary: Ivanti, Fortinet, SAP, VMware, and n8n have released security patches addressing vulnerabilities including authentication bypass and privilege escalation flaws. The most critical issue is CVE-2026-8043 (CVSS 9.6) affecting Ivanti Xtraction, which could enable information disclosure or client-side attacks.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud account credentials and internal system access details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked files reportedly detailed CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes, representing a significant credential and access management failure.

news.title: BeyondTrust Named an Overall Leader in 2026 KuppingerCole Analysts Leadership Compass for Privileged Access Management for Sixth Consecutive Year
news.summary: BeyondTrust has been named an Overall Leader in the 2026 KuppingerCole Leadership Compass for Privileged Access Management for the sixth consecutive year. The recognition spans Product, Innovation, and Market Leadership categories, with highlights including just-in-time and ephemeral access capabilities, AI agent governance, cross-domain privilege graph, and identity and DevOps integrations.

news.title: WinMagic Responds to New CISA OT Guidance With Transport-Layer Identity Architecture
news.summary: WinMagic has introduced a transport-layer identity architecture in response to new CISA guidance on operational technology (OT) environments. The solution features an endpoint-driven approach using Live Key and Live Identity technologies, addressing what the company says are gaps in current Zero Trust models for critical infrastructure where uptime and continuous trust are required.

news.title: GetReal Security Launches First Trust and Authenticity Platform That Combines Real-Time Continuous Identity Verification and Deepfake Detection
news.summary: GetReal Security has launched a platform called GetReal Protect that combines real-time continuous identity verification with deepfake detection across voice and video communications. The solution targets AI-generated fraud by authenticating and continuously verifying identity during live communications, addressing over $1.6 billion in reported deepfake-related financial losses in 2025.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a senior member of the cybercrime group Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft for his role in SMS phishing attacks against major technology companies in 2022. The attacks targeted authentication credentials through text-message phishing, enabling the group to compromise at least a dozen companies and steal tens of millions in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from users across more than 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, relying instead on router flaws to silently siphon authentication tokens at scale.
